ISSUE PAPER 
  
PROJECT:  ITEM: G-1 
    
   STAGE: 2 
  
REG. REF.:  Canadian Aviation Regulations (CARs) 
Part V, Subpart 21  
DATE: 2006 March 20 
  
NATIONAL 
POLICY REF.:  PL 500-002, Issue 1, Advisory 
Circular 500-016, Issue 1 
ISSUE STATUS:  Open 
  
SUBJECT: Type Certification Basis BRANCH ACTION: RAED, RACD, 
AARDC 
  
 COMPLIANCE 
TARGET:  Pre-Supplemental Type 
Certificate (STC) 
  
Basis of Certification 
 
STATEMENT OF ISSUE:   
Applicant and project description (general) 
 
BACKGROUND:    
Project details 
  
 
TRANSPORT CANADA POSITION:   
 
 
Certification Basis: 
Based upon the application date of 2010 XXXXXX, under the provisions of Canadian Aviation 
Regulations (CARs) 521.157, the applicable type certification standards for the modification to 
the XXXXXXX are as follows: 
 
Airworthiness & Environmental Standards for components and areas not affected by the 
change: 
 
In accordance with CAR 521.158 (4)(a), areas not affected by the change may meet:

title: PDF Page 2
content:
PROJECT:   C-0X-0XYZ ITEM: G-1 
MODEL:   XXXXXXXXXXXXX STAGE:  2 
                       DATE:  
 PAGE: 2 
 
The original certification basis for the Model XXX shown on TCDS XXX, Issue XX (FAA: 
XXXX, Revision XX). 
 
The XXXXX is certified in the Transport Category under TCCA Type Certificate XXX.  The 
certification basis is FAR 29 including amendments 29-1 and 29-2, plus 29.473, 29.501, 29.771, 
29.903c, 29.1323, and 29.1505b of amendment 29-3, along with FAA Special Condition number 
29-12-SW-1 and “Guidelines for Helicopter Certification Using Vertical Take-off Techniques 
From Ground Level and Elevated Heliports” vertical take-off criteria, dated 1971 February 3.  IFR 
instrument requirements for Bell Model 212 helicopters per SW-216 letter, dated 1970 July 1.  
Ditching:  FAR 29.801 of amendment 29-12 including FAR 29.1411 and 29.1415. 
Equivalent Safety finding for 29.501(e) One skid landing loads in the level attitude (FAA letter 
dated 1995 September 20). 
No exemptions. 
 
 
 
Airworthiness and Environmental Standards for components and areas affected
 by the 
change: 
 
In accordance with CAR 521.158 (3), and the associated guidance material in Advisory Circular 
500-016, Issue 1, this modification to the Model XXX has been determined to be SIGNIFICANT 
under the Changed Product Rule, requiring the changed aeronautical product to meet the 
provisions of CAR 521.158 (1) for standards of airworthiness, and CAR 521.159 for 
environmental standards. 
 
Therefore, unless otherwise noted, the modification is to be shown to comply with the following: 
 
1) 14 CFR part 29 (FAR 29), effective February 1, 1965, including Amdt. 29-47.  Based on 
analysis, the applicant should present to TCCA, each airworthiness requirement identified to be 
impractical or not contributing materially to the level of safety at the identified amendment level, 
in accordance with CAR 521.158 (4)(b) and the associated advisory material in Advisory Circular 
500-016, Issue 1, with specific reference to Appendix B. 
 
2) Special conditions will be proposed, as appropriate, in accordance with CAR 521.158 (7).  At 
this time no subjects have been identified as potential Special Conditions. 
 
3) Equivalent Safety Findings will be processed according to the provisions of CAR 521.30 
(1)(d). The applicant must request an equivalent safety finding and provide justification as 
necessary to show that an equivalent level of safety does, in fact, exist.  At this time, no subjects 
have been identified as potential equivalent safety findings. 
 
4) Exemptions from the applicable regulations will be processed in accordance with the 
provisions of the Aeronautics Act Section 5.9 (2).  The applicant must petition for an exemption.  
At this time no FAR or CAR sections have been identified as potential exemptions. 
 
5) Optional Design Regulations:

title: PDF Page 3
content:
PROJECT:   C-0X-0XYZ ITEM: G-1 
MODEL:   XXXXXXXXXXXXX STAGE:  2 
                       DATE:  
 PAGE: 3 
 
If the applicant wishes to retain or add design approvals which allow IFR and flight in icing 
conditions, the applicant will need to show that the original design approvals are unaffected by 
the proposed modification or that the rotorcraft as modified complies with the regulations at the 
amendment 29-47. 
 
6) Noise Standards CAR V, subpart 16, paragraph 516.01, that are in force on the date of 
application for the change in type design (reference CAR 521.159 (1)). 
 
7) Fuel Venting and Exhaust Emissions Standards CAR V, subpart 16, paragraph 516.02 
(venting), and CAR V, subpart 16, paragraph 516.03 (emissions) that are in force on the date of 
application for the change in type design (reference CAR 521.159 (3), (4)). 
 
Date of Application for Supplemental Type Certificate: 2010 XXXXXXX. 
 
 
Additional Design Requirements and Conditions
:   
Compliance with the established basis for type certification notwithstanding, CAR 521.160 
(3)(a) precludes issuance of a supplemental type certificate if there is any feature or characteristic 
that would make the product unsafe.  Should it be determined that such an unsafe feature or 
characteristic exists; the type certificate will be withheld pending acceptable action taken by the 
applicant to correct the unsafe feature or characteristic.  Unsafe features or characteristics will be 
identified and resolved using issue papers.  If requirements or conditions are used to correct the 
unsafe feature, they should be identified in the Supplemental Type Certificate Data Sheet in a 
section immediately following the certification basis.   
 
Compatibility of STC with previous Airworthiness Directives:
 
The applicant must review the airworthiness directives (ADs) applicable to the model being 
modified and determine if AD compliance is affected by their proposed modification.  If AD 
compliance is affected by the applicant's proposed modification, the applicant must request 
approval for an alternative method of compliance (AMOC) in accordance with AMOC 
provisions within the relevant AD.  The request should include an assessment of the effect of the 
modification on the unsafe condition addressed by the AD; and, if the unsafe condition has not 
been eliminated, the request should include specific proposed actions to address it.  Further, the 
instructions for continued airworthiness must be modified to include consideration of the 
AMOC. 
 
Compatibility of STC with previous incorporated STC(s)
  
The rotorcraft model applicability, identified in this STC, is in reference to the rotorcraft model 
configuration as delivered from the original equipment manufacturer.  It is incumbent on the 
applicant for this STC to show the altered aeronautical product (as defined by CAR 521.01), as 
modified by this STC, meets the applicable airworthiness regulations as required by CAR 
521.151.  This means that, if any modifications were incorporated since initial delivery of the 
rotorcraft that are effected by this STC, it is the responsibility of the applicant for this STC to 
demonstrate compatibility of this STC with the particular rotorcraft intended to be modified.  
Incompatibilities may occur in a number of areas: physical elements, procedural requirements, 
conflicting or similar limitations that when combined could result in more severe limitations.

title: PDF Page 4
content:
PROJECT:   C-0X-0XYZ ITEM: G-1 
MODEL:   XXXXXXXXXXXXX STAGE:  2 
                       DATE:  
 PAGE: 4 
 
 
Operating Certificate Requirements:    
CAR operating rules (parts VI, and VII) are occasionally amended to require compliance with 
newly adopted standards of CAR Standard 529 (FAR 29 equivalent) on a retroactive basis.  
Compliance with these newly adopted requirements is mandatory for operation under the 
applicable operating rules.  If not already included in the above certification basis, any such 
applicable CAR 529 (FAR 29) standards may be added to the type certification basis for the 
Model XXX for awareness, by mutual agreement between the applicant and TCCA.  However, it 
should be noted that compliance with any such retroactively required rules is required to operate 
under the applicable operating rules, whether or not those rules are specified in this issue paper 
as part of the certification basis. 
 
 
 
Changes to the Certification Basis
 
It is TCCA’s intent to identify the certification basis as completely and early as possible in the 
certification program by early closure of this G-1 issue paper.  Any changes to the certification 
basis due to the addition of new special conditions, elected compliance with later amendments, 
equivalent safety findings, exemptions, or due to the identification of an unsafe feature or 
characteristic will be coordinated with the applicant by a separate issue paper, and then added 
collectively to this G-1 in one update prior to the issuance of the Supplemental Type Certificate, 
such that the final certification basis will be summarized in the conclusion of this G-1 issue paper.  
 
A similar process will be used for proposal papers submitted under CAR 521.158 (4)(b) for 
individual FAR 29 paragraphs to be reviewed as impractical or not contributing materially to the 
level of safety at the amendment level identified above for the areas affected by the design change.

PROJECT:   C-0X-0XYZ ITEM: G-1 
MODEL:   XXXXXXXXXXXXX STAGE:  2 
                       DATE:  
 PAGE: 5 
 
 APPLICANT POSITION:  
 
 
Proposal 3: FAR 29.610 Lightning and static electricity protection 
 
Areas Affected
: 
XXXX 
 
 
Requirement History
: 
FAR 29.610 didn’t come into existence until Amendment 29-24. 
 
At amendment 29-24, applicant must protect rotorcraft against catastrophic effects from 
lightning by electrically bonding metallic components or by designing the components to 
minimize the effects of strike/divert the resulting electrical current. 
 
At amendment 29-40, 29.610d was added so that the applicant was required to minimize the 
accumulation of electrostatic charge, minimize the risk of shock to users, provide an electrical 
return path, and reduce to an acceptable level the effects on essential and electronic equipment. 
 
Proposal/Response
: 
Per TCDS, the XXX aircraft is certified to FAR 29.610 @ 29-2 (ie. compliance to FAR 29.610 
not shown).  It is proposed that the modified aircraft also be certified to FAR 29.610 @ 29-23.  It 
would be impractical to apply FAR 29.610 @ 29-40 for the following reasons:
